subject: deep-session-agent-count
insight: Deep sessions perform well with 5-18 agents across 4-5 waves (avg 10 agents, 4.5 waves). Sessions with fewer agents (5-8) still complete successfully but touch fewer files.
evidence: 10 deep sessions: agents=[9,17,18,15,7,13,7,5,8,6], waves=[4,4,5,5,5,5,4,4,5,4]. All achieved 100% completion with 0 failed agents. Session 3 (04-07): 25 agents across 8 waves (4 audit + 4 fix), 17 files, 76% completion.

subject: feature-session-sizing
insight: Feature sessions scale well: 10-11 agents across 4-5 waves handles 3-14 planned issues with 100% completion.
evidence: Session main-2026-04-05-2200: 10 agents, 4 waves, 11 files, 3/3 issues, 900s. Session main-2026-04-07-1600: 11 agents, 5 waves, 32 files, 14/14 issues, 100% completion.

subject: single-issue-focus
insight: Planning 1 issue per deep session yields 100% completion reliably. The codebase supports focused single-issue sessions with no carryover.
evidence: 6 deep sessions with effectiveness data all planned 1 issue (or 0+1 emergent) and achieved 100% completion with 0 carryover.

subject: batch-issue-sessions
insight: Batch sessions of 3-10 cohesive issues achieve 100% completion when issues are independent. 1 agent per issue parallelizes perfectly. Works across feature, deep, and housekeeping session types.
evidence: Session main-2026-04-06-1029: 3 issues (same file), 8 agents. Session main-2026-04-06-1830: 3 independent issues, 6 agents. Session main-2026-04-06-housekeeping3: 4 cross-repo cleanup tasks, 4 agents. Session main-2026-04-07-housekeeping: 5 issues, 5 agents, 1 wave, 100% completion. Session main-2026-04-07-2310: 3 issues, 3 agents, 2 waves, 100%.

subject: single-file-sequential-agents
insight: When all changes target a single file, use 1 agent per implementation wave to avoid merge conflicts. Sequential is faster than parallel + conflict resolution.
evidence: Session main-2026-04-06-1029: 3 issues all targeting scripts/run-quality-gate.sh. 1 Impl-Core agent did all 3 changes cleanly. No merge conflicts.

subject: cross-repo-cleanup-sizing
insight: Cross-repo housekeeping (git-rm, file deletion, config updates) works well with 3-4 agents and coordinator-driven checkpoints. Destructive operations need explicit user confirmation gates between waves.
evidence: Session main-2026-04-06-housekeeping3: BuchhaltGenie cleanup, 4 agents across 3 waves, 1425 files changed, 100% completion. User checkpoint before git-rm prevented accidental data loss.

subject: simplification-pass-catches-ai-slop
insight: Simplification pass consistently catches real dead code and over-documentation from AI-generated refactoring. AI agents produce slop comments, unnecessary try-catch, and redundant logic that a targeted cleanup pass removes.
evidence: Session 5 (04-06): caught dead code and over-docs in quality-gate refactor. Session 8 (04-06): removed AI slop comments from both bash scripts. Session 2 (04-04): simplification pass was highest-impact finding — couldn't work with strict enforcement.

subject: discovery-false-positive-rate
insight: Discovery probes have a ~50-60% noise rate (false positives + low-value findings). Agent findings must always be verified against source code before triaging to the user. Auto-deferring low-confidence findings and dual filtering (agent + coordinator) significantly reduces noise.
evidence: Session 5 (04-04): 8/13 = 62% false positives. Session 6 (04-06): 6/8 probes clean, only 2 real findings. Session 4 (04-06): 1 auto-deferred, 3/4 confirmed after verification. Session housekeeping (04-07): 11 raw, 9 verified, 4 dismissed = 55% noise.

subject: version-string-drift
insight: Version strings drift across docs files (marketplace.json, CONTRIBUTING.md, USER-GUIDE.md, README badge) between releases. A grep check for the old version string should be part of every finalization wave.
evidence: Session 4 (04-04): 3 stale version strings. Session 3 (04-06): marketplace.json lagged. Session 6 (04-06): version audit confirmed all aligned only after explicit check.

subject: lifecycle-simulation-finds-instruction-gaps
insight: Code-level lifecycle simulation (reading every skill and tracing data flow end-to-end) finds instruction gaps and contradictions that manual testing and running the system miss. Most effective for plugin/orchestration repos where 'running tests' doesn't cover instruction correctness.
evidence: Session 3 (04-06): 17 items found via skill-by-skill trace, 9 fixed. Session 2 (04-04): 15 issues found via 17-scenario tracing. Both found issues invisible to bash test suites. Session 3 (04-07): 37 gaps found via 3-platform trace, 28 fixed. Most thorough simulation yet.

subject: platform-abstraction-skill-layer-gap
insight: Scripts (platform.sh, enforce-scope.sh) handle all 3 platforms correctly, but Markdown skill instructions hardcode .claude/ paths. After adding a new platform, a dedicated pass to update ALL skill body text is required.
evidence: Session 3 (04-07): Lifecycle Sim v3 found 25+ hardcoded .claude/ refs across session-end (12), wave-executor (10), session-plan, evolve. Platform Notes existed but body text ignored them.
